name: Mom's Pineapple Upside-Down Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Grease three cast-iron skillets.
2. Drain pineapple rings, reserving juice.
3. Mix 1/2 cup butter with buttermilk and pineapple juice to form 1 1/2 cups liquid (mix equal parts if possible).
4. Place egg whites in a large bowl. Add 3 yolks to one small container and the remaining 3 yolks to another.
5. Prepare three 8-inch cake pans by greasing with shortening and lining with brown paper rounds.
6. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C).
7. In a large bowl, beat egg whites until stiff but not dry.
8. Alternately add flour mixture and remaining butter/sugar liquid to the egg white mixture in four steps, beginning and ending with flour.
9. Add vanilla extract and mix thoroughly.
10. Mix salt into the egg yolk mixture.
11. Combine all ingredients for topping: Melt butter in a large skillet over medium heat. Add pineapple juice and sugar, then water and Karo syrup. Cook at a high rolling boil for about 3 minutes.
12. Pour the topping mixture into prepared skillets (about 1/2 to 3/4-inch thick).
13. Place pineapple rings in the center of each skillet and add cherries.
14. Divide batter evenly among pans.
15. Bake at 350°F (175°C) for 30-35 minutes, or until done.
Note: Adjust cooking times as necessary based on your oven's performance.
